111540.explanation: In the new college of mayor and aldermen, we mainly want people with the right qualities. Whether there are more men or more women is of less importance.

111541.explanation: We find it important that residents can immediately participate in discussions about what they consider important. A referendum is a good option for this.

111543.explanation: Municipal Interests Buren believes that farmers should not be limited in their possibilities and that secondary activities should be encouraged.

111546.explanation: Municipal Interests Buren is against the arrival of a (large-scale) asylum seekers center (AZC) in our municipality.

111547.explanation: We want to help employers in the agriculture and horticulture sector house as many seasonal workers as possible on their own company premises by imposing as few restrictive rules as possible. We are against large-scale housing.

111549.explanation: Municipal Interests Buren is against imposing additional rules on top of national regulations.

111550.explanation: With the fireworks ban, a fireworks show can be a solution. It is important to us to first gauge the need for this among our residents.

111555.explanation: We believe everyone should be able to play sports. If residents cannot pay for this themselves, the municipality can help.

111556.explanation: As far as Municipal Interests Buren is concerned, no extra money is needed for the local broadcaster.

111559.explanation: We believe that sports clubs that are struggling should be helped. We want to develop sports complexes into vital sports parks. This turns sports clubs into meeting places for our residents. We want to invest in this.

111561.explanation: Safety is one of the main priorities of Municipal Interests Buren. If safety can be improved with more municipal enforcement officers, we certainly want to invest in this.

111562.explanation: Municipal Interests Buren wants to allow and deploy the use of camera surveillance in public places if less far-reaching measures prove insufficient to maintain public order. Cameras will only be used where truly necessary and no longer than necessary.

111566.explanation: We believe there must be enough homes for every resident. As long as that is not the case, vacation homes may be tolerated as a permanent residence.

111567.explanation: The starting point for Gemeentebelangen Buren is building within the existing village boundaries. However, we will also need to creatively explore possibilities outside these limits. We support the creation of a new residential area.

111568.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants residents to work within their capabilities. For example, we encourage residents receiving social assistance benefits to do volunteer work, provided it is possible.

111570.explanation: The municipality of Buren already applies a high income threshold. This does not need to be further increased.

111572.explanation: At the moment, we have designated sufficient parking spaces for electric cars. By designating even more, we would create parking problems for residents without electric vehicles.

111574.explanation: Residents have free choice of care. The municipality must ensure that the supply is such that residents can indeed make that choice themselves.

111578.explanation: We want to be a tourist-friendly municipality. Therefore, it is unwise to raise the tourist tax.

111579.explanation: First and foremost, we believe existing business parks should be renovated and vacancy addressed. If there is then more demand for business parks, of course these should be developed. We still have 14 hectares of space for this.

111580.explanation: We believe every entrepreneur should decide for themselves when they are open, including Sundays.

111582.explanation: Residents must be able to dispose of their waste at recycling centers. This helps prevent illegal dumping.

111583.explanation: As far as Gemeentebelangen Buren is concerned, we can fulfill our sustainability obligations in another way. Solar panels on fertile farmland are not necessary for this.

111586.explanation: We are not in favor of high-rise buildings. But to create enough homes for our residents that are also affordable, we will have to build multiple floors in some places.

111587.explanation: We do not allow renting out individual rooms in the villages due to possible abuse. The livability of our villages must not be jeopardized by buying up family homes and converting them into multiple-room rentals. However, this is permitted in the rural area.

111588.explanation: There is a need for more parking spaces, so these must indeed be realized.

111589.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Buren believes it is not feasible to make the center of the city of Buren car-free. A majority of residents also do not support this.

111590.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Buren wants a good use for the Royal Orphanage in Buren. However, the building does not belong to the municipality. Therefore investing money in it would not be desirable.

111591.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Buren is strongly opposed to this shelter on the border of our municipality. It only causes nuisance to our residents. The location is unsuitable. Therefore, we will not cooperate.

111592.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Buren is against more large wind turbines in our municipality. Therefore, we will not designate any locations for this.

111540.explanation: The executive board must be selected based on quality, not gender. We oppose quotas for men or women; we want just good, local governors for Buren.

111541.explanation: Resident participation is important, but decision-making also requires careful consideration. Referendums can be a tool for local decisions, not an end in themselves. The core agendas already serve as such a tool.

111543.explanation: We fully agree on selling local regional products. This offers farmers perspective when needed. Small-scale campsites like ‘camping at the farmer’s’ too. Larger side activities require tailor-made solutions. The municipality should collaborate but initiatives must fit the environment.

111546.explanation: Shelters require careful planning, community support, and distribution. The municipality must take responsibility, but with attention to livability and feasibility in our villages. A rural municipality naturally has different conditions and impacts than urban areas regarding shelters.

111547.explanation: Employers have a responsibility to provide good, appropriate housing, provided it is carefully integrated.

111549.explanation: Apart from the issues a ban might cause for the agricultural sector in the rural area, it is also a form of heat production that continues to work in emergencies.

111550.explanation: In our view, the municipality of Buren is too large to organize a local fireworks display at the expense of the entire community.

111555.explanation: Sports promote health and participation. Participation should not depend on income, but it should not be automatic either. It must be demonstrably financially unfeasible. We gladly make exceptions for children. Every child deserves the same opportunities.

111556.explanation: Local media strengthen engagement and information provision, but we find the current contribution more than sufficient.

111559.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for livability and health, but they must demonstrably meet a need. We do not support artificially keeping clubs alive with community funds.

111561.explanation: Visibility and safety are important, but efforts must be effective and targeted. If demonstrably necessary, then yes. But just scaling up without examining current problem areas is not useful in our opinion. More police on the street seems more useful from a prevention perspective.

111562.explanation: Camera surveillance can help enhance street safety and combat crime, but only where it is truly necessary and proportionate.

111566.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreation and contribute to the tourism and economic vitality of our municipality. Permanent residence undermines this character and often causes issues with facilities, enforcement, and livability. We solve the housing shortage through targeted housing construction.

111567.explanation: Building near and within existing villages is preferred to protect the landscape. If it really cannot be avoided and it contributes to solving the housing shortage, it should certainly be considered.

111568.explanation: Participation is important, but always in line with abilities and personal situations.

111570.explanation: Help must reflect the reality of rising costs.

111572.explanation: Sustainability requires good facilities, provided they are carefully integrated.

111574.explanation: Freedom of choice and respect for religion and culture are important in care. Any limitation in this can also affect the effectiveness of the treatment.

111578.explanation: As a municipality, we want to take good care of our entrepreneurs in the tourism sector, and in our view this does not include increasing taxes. Furthermore, this affects more people than just tourists, who we warmly welcome to our beautiful municipality.

111579.explanation: Employment and stimulating entrepreneurship are important, especially to keep entrepreneurs from Buren in Buren. But locations must be appropriate and small-scale.

111580.explanation: From a Christian perspective, we value Sunday as a day of rest which is good for everyone (believers and non-believers), and we also want to protect small entrepreneurs from unnecessarily higher costs and a society that demands they must always be open.

111582.explanation: Barriers to waste separation should remain low. This is better for the environment and reduces littering.

111583.explanation: We are against large-scale solar fields on fertile farmland.

111586.explanation: High-rise buildings do not suit a rural municipality like Buren.

111587.explanation: The number of single-person households is growing. Renting rooms offers an affordable housing option for this group. Room rentals typically cause few problems. Many tenants have steady jobs, have limited demands, and consciously choose this form of housing to save money.

111588.explanation: Accessibility is important, but tailored solutions per village are necessary. Public transport is not sufficiently accessible to everyone in our municipality. Therefore, cars are indispensable, and there must be enough parking spaces for them.

111589.explanation: Accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and especially the elderly must be maintained. It is fine if cars are 'guests', but mobility must be preserved.

111590.explanation: This heritage deserves careful, future-proof use. It is an icon for Buren and can definitely contribute to the local economy and livability. This does not mean a blank check; after all, we are not the owner, so clear performance conditions and agreements are necessary.

111591.explanation: Decisions must be made carefully, taking the environment and support base into account. Moreover, from a Christian perspective, we find it inexplicable to accommodate migrants precisely in this area—as far as possible away from social life and facilities.

111592.explanation: We oppose wind turbines that damage the landscape and livability. But if they must be built, we want the municipality itself to be able to decide where they are and are not allowed. We consider offshore wind much more effective than in the Betuwe region.

111540.explanation: VVD Buren opposes mandatory gender quotas. Leaders should be chosen based on quality, experience, and support, not on gender. Quotas limit freedom of choice and can exclude the best candidate.

111541.explanation: VVD Buren opposes referendums. Governing requires careful consideration of all interests. Elected council members are responsible for this; referendums oversimplify complex issues and undermine this role.

111543.explanation: VVD Buren believes additional activities on farms require a tailored approach. Not every location is suitable. Traffic pressure, livability, and nature must be considered; 'always allowing' is unwise.

111546.explanation: VVD Buren opposes an asylum seekers center (AZC) in our municipality. Reception is a national responsibility. An AZC puts pressure on housing, facilities, and support base, while local capacity is insufficient.

111547.explanation: VVD Buren believes that employers in agriculture and horticulture are responsible for proper housing of seasonal workers. Housing on their own premises can reduce pressure on the housing market and ensures clear responsibility.

111549.explanation: VVD Buren believes the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted. Residents should have freedom in their own homes, provided they burn responsibly and do not cause excessive nuisance.

111550.explanation: VVD Buren supports a municipal fireworks show. This provides a safe and collective alternative to private fireworks and contributes to village solidarity.

111555.explanation: VVD Buren opposes fully reimbursing sports memberships. Sports are important, but personal responsibility comes first. Targeted support is possible, but structural reimbursement goes too far.

111556.explanation: VVD Buren opposes extra municipal funding for the local broadcaster. Public money should go to core tasks such as security and facilities. The media should be as financially independent as possible.

111559.explanation: VVD Buren believes sports clubs are primarily responsible for their own finances. The municipality can support with facilities or advice, but structural financial support is not a core task.

111561.explanation: VVD Buren believes more enforcement officers are needed for safety and livability. More visible enforcement prevents nuisance, strengthens the feeling of safety, and relieves the police of local duties.

111562.explanation: VVD Buren believes additional camera surveillance can contribute to safety. Cameras help prevent and solve crime and enhance the feeling of security, provided their use is careful and respects privacy.

111566.explanation: VVD Buren opposes permanent residence in holiday homes. This undermines the tourist character, puts pressure on facilities, and circumvents rules on housing, safety, and spatial planning.

111567.explanation: To tackle the housing shortage, space is needed. With good integration, building outside the village center can be responsible and necessary.

111568.explanation: VVD Buren believes that requiring a contribution in exchange for welfare benefits is reasonable. Socially meaningful work promotes participation, increases self-esteem, and helps people towards employment, without displacing regular work.

111570.explanation: VVD Buren believes poverty aid should remain targeted at the lowest incomes. Raising the threshold makes schemes more expensive and less focused, while personal responsibility remains important.

111572.explanation: VVD Buren is cautious about adding public charging points. Parking pressure is already high. Charging is primarily the responsibility of car owners and market parties, not the municipality.

111574.explanation: VVD Buren believes good and accessible care for everyone is paramount. Freedom of choice is important, but care must remain affordable and available; it is not always possible to accommodate all religious or cultural wishes.

111578.explanation: VVD Buren opposes raising the tourist tax. This makes Buren less attractive to visitors and entrepreneurs, while tourism actually contributes to the local economy.

111579.explanation: VVD Buren believes more business parks are needed. This strengthens the local economy, creates jobs, and gives entrepreneurs room to grow, with attention to good integration and accessibility.

111580.explanation: VVD Buren opposes limiting Sunday shopping. Entrepreneurs should be able to decide when they are open, and consumers appreciate that freedom.

111582.explanation: VVD Buren believes the recycling center should remain free. This encourages responsible waste separation and prevents littering and illegal dumping, which is ultimately cheaper and better for the living environment.

111583.explanation: VVD Buren opposes a ban on solar panels on agricultural land. The energy transition requires space. With proper conditions, solar energy on farmland can contribute to sustainable energy without sealing everything off.

111586.explanation: VVD Buren is open to high residential flats where spatially appropriate. High-rise can contribute to housing construction, efficient use of space, and housing flow, provided quality and livability are guaranteed.

111587.explanation: VVD Buren believes room-by-room rental can help with the housing shortage. It increases supply, especially for starters and singles, provided nuisance is prevented and good rules apply.

111588.explanation: VVD Buren believes additional parking spaces are necessary. This improves accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs and reduces parking pressure.

111589.explanation: VVD Buren opposes a car-free center. Accessibility for entrepreneurs, the elderly, and disabled people must be maintained. A lively center requires good access, not closure.

111590.explanation: VVD Buren is cautious about investing in a new destination for the Orphanage. Public money should go to core tasks. Redevelopment is primarily the responsibility of the owner and market.

111591.explanation: VVD Buren believes Buren should not cooperate with this plan. The location is close to our municipality and impacts livability and support base. Decisions about reception must be made carefully and in consultation with residents.

111592.explanation: VVD Buren opposes designating locations for wind turbines. They have a major impact on the landscape and living environment. Sustainable energy should first be sought in alternatives with less nuisance.

111540.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

111541.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

111543.explanation: Agriculture must become more sustainable, but we also want farmers to be able to earn money with their businesses. A shop with good products from their own land or a nice campsite can help. Therefore, we want to make this possible. Clear rules and input from local residents are preconditions.

111546.explanation: PvdA-GroenLinks stands for reception and solidarity and believes that the municipality of Buren must take responsibility for its contribution to the national reception in accordance with the Distribution Act, but the form, location, and scale must be carefully determined and as much as possible in cooperation with neighboring municipalities.

111547.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must ensure these workers have decent housing without burdening our residents. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in our municipality.

111549.explanation: We do believe that nuisance and unhealthy situations should be limited, for example by managing the times when burning is harmful (such as through the Burning Guide) and by promoting conscious and responsible burning. We want to discourage burning in densely populated centers.

111550.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

111555.explanation: Sport promotes health and social connection. The municipality must remove obstacles.

111556.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the different neighborhoods to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to stay afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

111559.explanation: Exercising gives a lot of energy and satisfaction. It keeps you healthy and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club shuts down, an entire neighborhood suffers. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in if necessary. This should come with rules.

111561.explanation: Enforcement is needed for traffic safety, spray-free zones, and livability.

111562.explanation: It is very important to feel safe on the street. Cameras can help in places where things really go wrong, but we also protect residents' privacy. We prefer to deploy more enforcement officers who can immediately intervene if something goes wrong.

111566.explanation: Can help alleviate housing shortage but must not lead to deterioration or loss of recreation areas.

111567.explanation: Housing construction must be focused on the centers and appropriate, not at the expense of the landscape. We guard against too much densification in the cores, so limited new construction outside the centers is allowed. However, this option is secondary.

111568.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

111570.explanation: More residents gain access to support, preventing debt and stress.

111572.explanation: Fits with a fair energy transition and sustainable mobility. Charging at one's own home must be made more possible, for example through a charging channel.

111574.explanation: Care must align with diversity and personal beliefs.

111578.explanation: Tourism may contribute to the maintenance of facilities and nature. The current tax is the lowest in the entire region. Tax should be indexed, just like all other taxes.

111579.explanation: We choose a small-scale, green economy. New companies should be housed as much as possible on existing sites. Companies within centers that cause nuisance should be relocated to a business park. If it turns out we have too little space, we look for a new location.

111580.explanation: Local entrepreneurs and residents must have freedom. We believe it is important that shops can open on Sundays for both shopkeepers and customers who want that. This also makes it much easier for singles who work during the week to do important shopping.

111582.explanation: Stimulates waste separation and prevents dumping.

111583.explanation: We want to generate sustainable energy in Buren, but not at the expense of our fertile agricultural land. That is important for food production and our landscape. Therefore, we mainly focus on solar panels on roofs and business parks. A complete ban on agricultural land is too far, but our clear preference is: first use what is already built to protect our precious land.

111586.explanation: By high residential flats, we mean buildings of six floors or higher. Such high-rise buildings do not fit the village character and open landscape of Buren. Limited height is allowed, up to about four floors, provided it is well integrated.

111587.explanation: Can alleviate housing shortage but must not lead to nuisance or poor living quality.

111588.explanation: Accessibility is important, but the focus is on public transport, car sharing, and cycle safety. Parking spaces take up public space. We are careful with this. Parking spaces only lead to more car ownership.

111589.explanation: Increases livability, safety, and space for social interaction.

111590.explanation: The Weeshuis is an important part of Buren's cultural heritage. We believe it must retain a public function and that the municipality should play an active role in finding a new, future-proof destination for this monumental building.

111591.explanation: Cooperation and solidarity are important, but location and purpose must be carefully weighed.

111592.explanation: Fits with an energy-neutral municipality, provided the location is carefully chosen and residents have a say.

111541.explanation: However, the topic must concern all residents of the Municipality of Buren.

111543.explanation: Provided it fits within the zoning plans.

111546.explanation: We consider the Municipality of Buren not a suitable place for this. We are much stronger in facilitating small-scale shelter.

111547.explanation: Seasonal workers also have the right to good housing. Many entrepreneurs already provide this on their own premises or at a suitable location.

111549.explanation: Especially given the available fuel in the region, which can be used locally in this way.

111550.explanation: Rather, the municipality should offer the possibility to give this shape per district.

111555.explanation: Sport is an important part of a healthy lifestyle and should be available to everyone in this way.

111556.explanation: Local broadcasting is already supported and should, if necessary, raise additional funds itself, for example through selling advertising time.

111559.explanation: The municipality can play a connecting role in such a situation, between associations internally (e.g., via the Burense Sport Cooperative).

111561.explanation: In this, the necessity and the possibilities for financing must be looked at.

111562.explanation: Enforce at the current level and on the grounds for this.

111566.explanation: These homes are intended to promote the recreation sector, remain available for recreation, and have been set up accordingly.

111567.explanation: New construction should be made possible where it is appropriate.

111568.explanation: Do not impose work, but by putting them in contact with the Volunteers Platform Buren they may be able to make a suitable social contribution.

111570.explanation: National standards can be applied for this.

111572.explanation: When a need arises in the future, it should be responded to.

111574.explanation: Providing care is a top priority.

111578.explanation: We want to provide more space for recreational activities. The intensification of these also brings increased pressure on the facilities. To maintain these at a good level, funds will need to be secured, for example through the tourist tax.

111579.explanation: There is still enough room for expansion on the current sites. If more demand arises in the future, possibilities can be explored at that time.

111580.explanation: Since these do not exist in the municipality of Buren, they cannot be reduced either.

111582.explanation: The processing of our waste involves high costs, which where necessary should continue to be charged to the provider. This also contributes to proper waste separation.

111583.explanation: Good agricultural land must remain available with regard to our food supply and should not be used for this purpose. Alternatives locations for additional solar panels should be considered first. However, it remains up to the landowner; a ban would go too far.

111586.explanation: In harmony with the landscape and surroundings.

111587.explanation: Gives young people the opportunity to live independently earlier.

111588.explanation: Alternative parking spaces around the city of Buren should be considered earlier.

111589.explanation: For the accessibility of retailers in the city of Buren and the residents themselves, this does not seem to us to be a desirable situation.

111590.explanation: The responsibility to give this building a new function lies with the owners. Unfortunately, the Municipality of Buren has no say in this.

111591.explanation: We will, in any case, need to continue the conversation with each other about these plans.

111592.explanation: In harmony with the landscape and surroundings. Participation of local residents is of great importance.

111540.explanation: 'Must' is too strong. D66 wants a good balance between men and women. But unique qualities and ideas are not tied to being male or female. Everyone is also an individual, not a representative of a group. By deploying various good people, the municipality can make better choices.

111541.explanation: D66 finds it important that residents can participate in discussions and decisions. With major and important issues such as new housing, facilities for the community, and good education, everyone's voice must clearly count. This can be done, for example, via a referendum, provided it is well prepared.

111543.explanation: D66 sees opportunities for farmers to diversify their businesses, such as with a shop or campsite. We are definitely positive about this, as it strengthens our beautiful environment. But the plans must be sustainable and not cause nuisance. Therefore, 'always allow' is too strong.

111546.explanation: D66 stands for humane and fair reception, spread throughout the Netherlands. Buren must and can also contribute. A well-organized asylum seeker center fits our responsibility and can be an enrichment for the municipality.

111547.explanation: D66 believes employers should take responsibility for good housing, with clear rules and attention to livability. This enables the valued contribution of seasonal workers without nuisance for other residents. This helps entrepreneurs.

111549.explanation: Wood burning is certainly cozy, but also a source of fine particulate matter, toxic substances, and sometimes health complaints. Therefore, clear agreements must be in place. Wood burning must not cause nuisance in the neighborhood. Everyone should be able to live together comfortably.

111550.explanation: 'Must' is too strong. Only if there is sufficient demand within Buren for a municipal fireworks show does D66 consider this a good idea. It is indeed a unifying alternative to still enjoy fireworks together now that the nationwide fireworks ban has been imposed.

111555.explanation: D66 wants sports for everyone. Playing sports helps health and prevents healthcare costs in the long term. Sports also provide social connection, which is very important within our municipality. Support for people with social benefits therefore helps to participate and stay healthy.

111556.explanation: Journalism, including local journalism, strengthens democracy and involvement. It also ensures the municipality does its work better. The question is whether additional funding for the local broadcaster is most needed or whether, for example, a broader mix of channels would be more helpful.

111559.explanation: Sports clubs have an important social role in our communities. Sports also improve the health of our children and other residents. If targeted subsidies help sports clubs to continue existing, that is certainly an investment worth making.

111561.explanation: D66 chooses visible and approachable enforcement officers to enhance safety and livability. Community enforcement officers can help improve the sense of security, allowing the police to focus on serious crime and undermining activities.

111562.explanation: D66 sees camera surveillance in our municipality only as a supplement, not a solution. Only very targeted, if the majority of local residents express a need for it, and with regard for privacy.

111566.explanation: Permanent residence will only be made possible where it fits the environment and has sufficient access roads and facilities. Buildings must also be safe to live in. D66 does not want a repeat of the inadequate enforcement of holiday parks in the past.

111567.explanation: D66 primarily builds within community centers. This also strengthens facilities (shops, sports clubs) within these centers. Currently, Buren builds far too few homes. Therefore, options outside the center are also negotiable. Think of a small group of houses on farms if they see value in that.

111568.explanation: D66 believes in participation according to ability, voluntary and meaningful. So the Municipality of Buren can certainly encourage volunteer work. But the municipality should not compel or punish. That doesn’t work.

111570.explanation: D66 wants to help more people. A higher income limit prevents some people from falling between the cracks. The help must be tailored and provide a long-term solution, such as investment in education, health, and participation. Not just direct income support.

111572.explanation: D66 wants to stimulate clean transportation. Not everyone has their own driveway, however. For them, charging points make electric driving accessible. Often this happens on initiative of residents, and such initiatives are definitely supported by D66.

111574.explanation: D66 values freedom of choice and care should fit who someone is and what they need. But, for example, home care is already scarce, and providing good quality care for everyone is more important. So people should certainly be able to choose where possible, but 'always' is too strong.

111578.explanation: The tourist tax in Buren is now low compared to the region, so a reasonable increase may be appropriate. D66 wants to use the revenue to invest in making Buren even more attractive, including sufficient culture, preserving historic buildings, and beautiful, accessible nature.

111579.explanation: D66 wants space for local businesses, with attention to sustainability and the environment. Where space is currently lacking, this should be made possible. In some communities, vacant buildings can better be used first.

111580.explanation: D66 finds it unfortunate that entrepreneurs in Buren are limited. Some stores want to be open on Sundays and possibly closed on other days of the week. D66 believes this should be possible.

111582.explanation: D66 wants to encourage waste separation. Free access to the recycling center helps with that.

111583.explanation: The possibility of solar panels gives landowners more options to make the best use of their land, and helps ensure a sustainable future for us and our children. Of course, we assess per location if and how this fits the surroundings, but not banning everything in advance.

111586.explanation: Only if taller buildings fit within the community, is D66 Buren open to higher construction. This can also help us create more homes for future generations. Naturally, it should not detract too much from the current residents' living enjoyment, so it remains tailored per location.

111587.explanation: D66 wants to look at multiple ways to quickly realize more housing. Where livability and safety are guaranteed, renting out part of the home should be possible.

111588.explanation: D66 chooses smart mobility. In some places extra parking spaces are needed, in others not.

111589.explanation: Traffic in the historic center of the Buren community can be much safer and more pleasant for pedestrians. This is mainly due to 'through traffic' cars, not residents driving to or from their homes in the center. So not completely car-free, but only cars as destination traffic.

111590.explanation: The future of Het Weeshuis is important for the municipality. If a new destination becomes possible through a municipal contribution, this should be open for discussion. This contribution is likely to be more in the form of advice, assistance, permits, and (parking) facilities rather than direct funding.

111591.explanation: A “migrant village” near our municipal border does not sound attractive. However, opposing our neighboring municipality is counterproductive. It is better to collaborate to ensure careful decisions, with attention to humanity and support. This way, we can actually reduce nuisance.

111592.explanation: D66 wants sustainable energy with proper participation from residents. The province designated a location because the municipality of Buren did not dare to take on this task. Now the municipality is challenging this. D66 does not want to keep spending even more on this but prefers to use the funds to help the local residents.

111540.explanation: The municipal government and the civil service should reflect a balanced representation of society. Buren is investigating how its own organization, including the mayor and aldermen, can become more inclusive and diverse.

111541.explanation: Instead of making plans for residents, we make plans with residents. Citizens' assemblies play an important role in participation policy. Referendums have a place in major decisions. Using multiple-choice questions prevents oversimplification by avoiding only yes or no options.

111543.explanation: We support strengthening the local economy (organic fruit/vegetables), but it must not come at the expense of a nature reserve (for example, a campsite) or animal welfare.

111546.explanation: Buren takes a fair share in providing shelter for refugees, together with residents and the COA. We choose small-scale, humane reception that helps asylum seekers integrate faster.

111547.explanation: We want fair housing for labor migrants. Employers must take responsibility, but housing on the employer’s own site makes labor migrants too dependent on their employer.

111549.explanation: Cutting down nature to burn wood is not sustainable. For the health of people and animals, wood stoves should no longer be used.

111550.explanation: Fireworks are not necessary to celebrate a festival. Festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives to fireworks, such as shows with light, music, drones, and lasers, can take place as long as people, animals, nature, and the environment do not suffer harm.

111555.explanation: Sports must be accessible and safe for everyone. People receiving social benefits should also be able to participate in sports and not become isolated.

111556.explanation: Strong, independent local media and regional broadcasters are very important for the residents of Buren. No cuts will be made here. To promote independent (investigative) journalism, an accessible local media fund for non-commercial local media will be established.

111559.explanation: In a municipality where people live spread out, it is important for social cohesion that sports clubs can continue to exist.

111561.explanation: Buren invests in more street workers, neighborhood parents, and district police officers, the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. Thanks to their efforts, possible problems in the neighborhood can be detected early before they escalate.

111562.explanation: We do not opt for more camera surveillance. Cameras often offer a false sense of security and infringe on the privacy of residents in this municipality.

111566.explanation: We ensure that available homes are better utilized. Holiday homes in the municipality of Buren are mainly located in our valuable nature areas. (Holiday) homes in these vulnerable places, such as floodplains or deep polders, do not have our support.

111567.explanation: We ensure that nature and landscape are preserved. New homes will be built within existing residential areas, not outside them.

111568.explanation: We want to motivate people on welfare, not make it compulsory. Volunteering and informal care are encouraged, but only if someone wants and is able to do so.

111570.explanation: No one should have to stretch their budget every month with the stress of whether there is enough for groceries. We raise the support threshold to 130% of the social minimum. This gives more households financial breathing room and the opportunity to build a buffer for unforeseen expenses.

111572.explanation: Buren should allow the number of charging points for electric cars to grow proportionally with the number of electric cars in the municipality. Additionally, Buren is committed to so-called 'green' parking spaces, such as those with grass pavers.

111574.explanation: We choose care that fits the person. Where reasonable, caregivers take into account faith, culture, and identity.

111578.explanation: We choose to use tourist tax for sustainability. An increase provides more funds for nature and living environment and compensates any damage.

111579.explanation: We ensure that space in Buren is used smartly. By improving existing sites, there remains room for housing and greenery. We want (fruit) storage buildings to move to industrial areas, so that space becomes available for housing in village centers.

111580.explanation: The Party for the Animals strives to find a balance between economic activity and social and ecological responsibilities. A 24/7 economy is not suitable for employees and small entrepreneurs. It goes without saying that closing on a Sunday should not necessarily be mandatory.

111582.explanation: The Party for the Animals applies the principle 'the polluter pays.' We want less waste and more reuse. Therefore, collecting 1 m³ of bulky waste remains free, and we stimulate repair cafés, thrift stores, and reuse.

111583.explanation: Agricultural land is not intended for solar parks but for plant-based food production and nature development and, if livestock farming shrinks significantly, for housing.

111586.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to tackle the housing shortage in a sustainable way. Fully building out Buren is not the solution; that would make the municipality unlivable. We want to fairly, sustainably, and affordably distribute the available space and homes, while maintaining the rural character.

111587.explanation: Dividing houses into multiple units and/or splitting them can sometimes be a solution for the shortage of suitable homes. It must be assessed per village whether this fits the environment and it must not come at the expense of public space. Splitting must comply with clear rules to prevent abuses.

111588.explanation: To reduce traffic pressure in village centers as well as streets full of parked cars, more parking facilities on the edges of the centers can be a solution.

111589.explanation: Where possible, Buren chooses a car-free village center. Residents can park in parking facilities on the edges. Visitors to Buren’s inner city are encouraged to come by bike or public transport.

111590.explanation: Buren promotes the repurposing of monuments. Demolition of (cultural) historically valuable or iconic buildings should be prevented. The municipality should proactively seek subsidies for national monuments.

111591.explanation: The municipality has a humane reception policy for people seeking safe shelter and treats them equally. This is based on inclusion, living together, housing, and full participation, rather than the outdated concept of 'integration.' The intended location does not meet our criteria.

111592.explanation: Buren stimulates wind energy in locations where people, animals, and nature experience little or no nuisance, for example along highways and on industrial sites. No wind turbines will be placed in or near nature reserves or in or near areas with vulnerable bird or bat populations.

111541.explanation: If there is sufficient support from the residents, a referendum should be possible.

111543.explanation: BBB wants to support local entrepreneurship and short supply chains.

111546.explanation: BBB is against a refugee center in the municipality of Buren.

111547.explanation: Housing for labor migrants preferably as close as possible to the workplace.

111549.explanation: Residents decide for themselves how they heat their homes.

111550.explanation: The municipality must facilitate that third parties can organize a fireworks display.

111555.explanation: Everyone should have access to sports. Maintain the existing arrangement.

111556.explanation: Local broadcasters are financed by the national government (from 2026) and advertising revenue.

111559.explanation: Occasional support should be possible, because sports clubs play an important role in the livability of a community.

111561.explanation: More police on the street, but the municipal council has no say in that. Only if more police are available than the deployment of enforcement officers.

111562.explanation: Camera surveillance can create a false sense of security in certain situations. It works preventively in some cases.

111566.explanation: Tourism is an important source of income for the municipality.

111567.explanation: Where possible (location), the municipality should allow the destination of a site to be changed.

111568.explanation: Volunteering can also prevent social isolation.

111570.explanation: Maintain the current limit of 120% of the social assistance level.

111572.explanation: Maintain current policy, designate parking spaces at locations where car users cannot provide their own charging points.

111574.explanation: People should be able to choose their own healthcare provider.

111579.explanation: Designate new business parks based on the market situation.

111580.explanation: The entrepreneur decides the shopping Sundays themselves.

111583.explanation: There is plenty of space for solar panels on roofs.

111586.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit our landscape. Maximum 3 floors.

111587.explanation: The housing shortage requires a flexible solution.

111588.explanation: Designate parking locations based on demand.

111589.explanation: Seek a solution through citizen participation and/or organize a referendum.

111590.explanation: The town of Buren needs a new location for the functions of village hall, exhibitions, events, etc.

111591.explanation: BBB is against a large-scale asylum seekers center (azc) in the municipality or its immediate vicinity.

111592.explanation: Only room for initiatives from residents for small wind turbines.

statements.title: Buren must designate a site where wind turbines may be built.

titleParts.text: Buren moet een plek aanwijzen waar

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain allowed in Buren.

titleParts.text: In Buren moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Buren must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The residents of Buren must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Buren moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Buren.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality of Buren must not cooperate with Neder-Betuwe’s plan to build migrant accommodation on the Bredesteeg.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Buren moet niet meewerken aan het plan van de gemeente Neder-Betuwe om een opvang voor

titleParts.glossary: People who have come to the Netherlands from another country, for any reason.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Buren must create more business parks.
The center of Buren must become car-free.
Tall residential apartment buildings may be built in Buren.
Dropping off waste at the recycling centre should remain free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: There must be more parking spaces in Buren.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must allow fewer Sunday shopping days.
The municipality must invest money in a new use for Het Koninklijk Weeshuis in Buren.
